title: Assistant Interaction Baseline
description: End-to-end Roca assistant interaction scenarios with mocked agents, projects, approvals, speech, diagnostics, and transcript assertions.

scenarios.title: Codex endpoint lookup
scenarios.description: Codex handoff should feel conversational, hide raw tool progress, speak only short summaries, and put endpoint details in Markdown.

turns.user: Can you ask sample-auth in Codex what endpoints there are for logins?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found 2 login endpoints. I put the list below.","detailsMarkdown":"## Login endpoints\n- POST /v1/auth/login/login/begin\n- POST /v1/auth/login/login/finish"}

messages.text: I'll ask Codex to inspect Sample Auth and summarize what it finds.
Codex finished.
Codex found 2 login endpoints. I put the list below.

spokenTexts: I'll ask Codex to inspect Sample Auth and summarize what it finds.
Codex found 2 login endpoints. I put the list below.

scenarios.title: Developer workflow architecture summary
scenarios.description: An explicit providerless repo architecture request should use Roca's local Codebase Skill and return a concise Roca summary with details.

localSkill.evidenceMarkdown:
# Codebase Skill Evidence
## Top-Level Files
- RocaMac
- Packages/RocaCore
- Packages/RocaServices
- Packages/RocaProviders

turns.user: In the Roca repo, summarize architecture and important entry points.

brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"I inspected Roca locally and found the main entry points.","detailsMarkdown":"## Entry points
- RocaMac/App
- Packages/RocaCore
- Packages/RocaServices
- Packages/RocaProviders"}

messages.text: I'll inspect Roca locally.
Local scan complete.
I inspected Roca locally and found the main entry points.

spokenTexts: I inspected Roca locally and found the main entry points.

scenarios.title: Developer workflow implementation plan
scenarios.description: An explicit providerless repo implementation-plan request should use Roca's local Codebase Skill in plan mode.

localSkill.evidenceMarkdown:
# Codebase Skill Evidence
## Search Results
- routes/login.ts
- services/loginRecovery.ts

## Notes
Plan should cover tradeoffs and tests.

turns.user: For the Sample Auth repo, draft an implementation plan for login recovery and tradeoffs.

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runSkill","skillID":"codebase","projectName":"sample-auth","prompt":"draft an implementation plan for login recovery with tradeoffs","mode":"ask"}
brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"I drafted the local login recovery plan.","detailsMarkdown":"## Plan
- Inspect login recovery routes.
- Map service boundaries.
- Add focused tests.

## Tradeoffs
- Keep recovery small for alpha, then expand."}

messages.text: I'll inspect Sample Auth locally and draft a concise plan.
Local scan complete.
I drafted the local login recovery plan.

spokenTexts: I'll inspect Sample Auth locally and draft a concise plan.
I drafted the local login recovery plan.

scenarios.title: Spreadsheet filtered average
scenarios.description: A local spreadsheet question should route to Spreadsheet Skill, keep speech concise, and put calculation provenance in details.

localSkill.evidenceMarkdown:
# Spreadsheet Skill Evidence
## Average
The average of Amount is 200 across 2 numeric row(s).

Operation: average
Value column: Amount
Matching rows: 2
Numeric rows used: 2
Filters: Status = Paid

## Preview: sales
| Region | Status | Amount |
|---|---|---|
| North | Paid | 100 |
| North | Paid | 300 |

turns.user: In the sales report spreadsheet, what is the average amount where status is paid?

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runSkill","skillID":"spreadsheet","projectName":"sales report","prompt":"calculate the average amount where status is paid","mode":"ask"}
brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"The paid average amount is 200 across 2 numeric rows.","detailsMarkdown":"## Calculation
- Sheet: sales
- Value column: Amount
- Filter: Status = Paid
- Matching rows: 2
- Numeric rows used: 2"}

messages.text: I'll analyze Sales Report locally and show the calculation.
Spreadsheet Skill finished.
The paid average amount is 200 across 2 numeric rows.

spokenTexts: I'll analyze Sales Report locally and show the calculation.
The paid average amount is 200 across 2 numeric rows.

scenarios.title: Codebase nested infrastructure follow-up
scenarios.description: A local codebase conversation should keep project context, narrow to infrastructure on follow-up, and avoid generic repeated intro copy.

localSkill.evidenceMarkdown:
# Codebase Skill Evidence
## Repository Map
- go.mod (Go module)
- infra/package.json (Node package)
- infra/cdk.json (AWS CDK app)

## Targeted File Evidence
### `infra/package.json`
```text
{"type":"module","dependencies":{"aws-cdk-lib":"^2.0.0"}}
```

### `infra/bin/infra.js`
```text
import { App } from 'aws-cdk-lib';
```

turns.user: In the Sample Auth repo, what language is the project in?

brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"Sample Auth's main app evidence points to Go, with separate JavaScript CDK infrastructure under infra.","detailsMarkdown":"## Evidence
- `go.mod`: Go module
- `infra/package.json`: JavaScript/Node package
- `infra/cdk.json`: AWS CDK app"}

messages.text: I'll check Sample Auth's languages and project structure.
Local scan complete.
Sample Auth's main app evidence points to Go, with separate JavaScript CDK infrastructure under infra.

spokenTexts: I'll check Sample Auth's languages and project structure.
Sample Auth's main app evidence points to Go, with separate JavaScript CDK infrastructure under infra.

skillRequests.promptContains: what language is the project in?

turns.user: What about its infrastructure code?

brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"The deployment infrastructure evidence points to JavaScript AWS CDK under infra.","detailsMarkdown":"## Evidence
- `infra/package.json`: Node package with `aws-cdk-lib`
- `infra/cdk.json`: CDK app config
- `infra/bin/infra.js`: CDK entry point"}

messages.text: Got it, I'll look specifically at Sample Auth's infrastructure code.
Local scan complete.
The deployment infrastructure evidence points to JavaScript AWS CDK under infra.

spokenTexts: Got it, I'll look specifically at Sample Auth's infrastructure code.
The deployment infrastructure evidence points to JavaScript AWS CDK under infra.

turns.user: No I mean the deployment infra stuff. It should be in the infra folder in that repo.

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runSkill","skillID":"codebase","prompt":"inspect deployment infra in the infra folder","mode":"ask"}
brain.responseText:
{"bubbleText":"You were right, the deployment infra evidence is JavaScript CDK in the infra folder.","detailsMarkdown":"## Evidence
- `infra/package.json`: Node package
- `infra/cdk.json`: AWS CDK app
- `infra/bin/infra.js`: CDK entry point"}

messages.text: Got it, I'll look specifically at Sample Auth's infrastructure code.
Local scan complete.
You were right, the deployment infra evidence is JavaScript CDK in the infra folder.

spokenTexts: Got it, I'll look specifically at Sample Auth's infrastructure code.
You were right, the deployment infra evidence is JavaScript CDK in the infra folder.

skillRequests.promptContains: inspect deployment infra in the infra folder

scenarios.title: Codebase partial evidence absence follow-up
scenarios.description: When prior codebase evidence is partial and the user asks an absence-style factual follow-up, Roca should verify with the Codebase Skill instead of answering from a thin prior summary.

localSkill.evidenceMarkdown:
# Codebase Skill Evidence
## Repository Map
```text
- Files scanned: 10
```

## Evidence Contract
Roca inspected a bounded subset. Treat absence claims as uncertain.

turns.user: What language is the nova-backend project in?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Nova Backend is mostly Go.","detailsMarkdown":null}

messages.text: Nova Backend is mostly Go.

skillRequests.promptContains: what language is the project in?

turns.user: So no JavaScript then?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"I checked again. Nova Backend has JavaScript infrastructure too.","detailsMarkdown":"## Evidence\n- `infra/package.json`"}

messages.text: I checked again. Nova Backend has JavaScript infrastructure too.

scenarios.title: Codex act intro copy
scenarios.description: Edit requests should say Roca is asking Codex to make a change, not inspect the project.

turns.user: Ask Codex to add a short comment to the Sample Auth login route.

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","projectName":"sample-auth","prompt":"add a short comment to the login route","mode":"act"}
brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex updated the login route comment.","detailsMarkdown":"Changed routes/login.ts."}

messages.text: I'll ask Codex to make that change in Sample Auth and summarize what changed.
Codex updated the login route comment. Changed file: routes/login.ts.

forbiddenMessageTextContains: inspect Sample Auth and summarize what it finds
spokenTexts: I'll ask Codex to make that change in Sample Auth and summarize what changed.
Codex updated the login route comment. Changed file: routes/login.ts.

scenarios.title: Read previous agent result out loud
scenarios.description: Follow-up requests to say the previous Codex result should use chat memory, not selected-text reading.

turns.user: Ask Codex for the Sample Auth login endpoints.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found 2 login endpoints.","detailsMarkdown":"## Endpoints\n- POST /v1/auth/login/login/begin\n- POST /v1/auth/login/login/finish"}

turns.user: Can you tell it to me out loud?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found two Sample Auth login endpoints: one to begin login and one to finish login.","detailsMarkdown":null}

spokenTexts: Codex found two Sample Auth login endpoints: one to begin login and one to finish login.

scenarios.title: Verbal endpoint flow
scenarios.description: When the user asks for a spoken flow, Roca should explain it conversationally instead of printing a Markdown artifact.

turns.user: Can you tell me the flow for the endpoints, not just print them out?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Sure. The flow is: start the secure login, let the client complete the platform challenge, then finish the login with the signed result.","detailsMarkdown":null}

spokenTexts: Sure. The flow is: start the secure login, let the client complete the platform challenge, then finish the login with the signed result.

scenarios.title: Codex edit approval card
scenarios.description: Approval prompts should render as actionable chat cards with approve-once/session/deny outcomes.

turns.user: Codex wants approval to edit Sample Auth.

messages.text: Remembered approval.

scenarios.title: Follow-up edit context
scenarios.description: A follow-up edit should keep the prior provider and project context rather than asking the user to restate the folder.

turns.user: Ask Codex in Sample Auth what login files matter.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found the login files.","detailsMarkdown":"## Files\n- routes/login.ts"}

turns.user: Great, tell Codex to add a short comment there.

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","prompt":"add a short comment in the login files","mode":"act"}
brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex updated the login file with a short comment.","detailsMarkdown":"Changed routes/login.ts."}

scenarios.title: Ambiguous project clarification
scenarios.description: Ambiguous project phrases should produce a concise project-choice question before any agent is started.

turns.user: Ask Codex in the nova project what auth endpoints exist.

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Frontend?

scenarios.title: Project clarification resumes original request
scenarios.description: After Roca asks which project the user means, a precise project answer should resume the original agent request instead of starting a new chat turn.

turns.user: Can you ask codex to tell me how many lines the README.md file is in the nova project?

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","projectName":"nova","prompt":"tell me how many lines the README.md file is","mode":"ask"}

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Mailer?

turns.user: nova backend

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found the README line count.","detailsMarkdown":"README.md has 42 lines."}

messages.text: I'll ask Codex to inspect Nova Backend and summarize what it finds.
Codex found the README line count.

scenarios.title: Project clarification answer is still ambiguous
scenarios.description: If the clarification answer is still ambiguous, Roca should ask again without starting the agent.

turns.user: Can you ask codex to tell me how many lines the README.md file is in the nova project?

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","projectName":"nova","prompt":"tell me how many lines the README.md file is","mode":"ask"}

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Mailer?

turns.user: nova

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Mailer?

scenarios.title: Unrelated follow-up clears project clarification
scenarios.description: A short unrelated command after a project clarification should not be misread as a project answer.

turns.user: Can you ask codex to tell me how many lines the README.md file is in the nova project?

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","projectName":"nova","prompt":"tell me how many lines the README.md file is","mode":"ask"}

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Mailer?

turns.user: open safari

messages.text: Opened App.

forbiddenMessageTextContains: I couldn't match that to one of the project options

scenarios.title: Explicit ambiguous project beats prior context
scenarios.description: If recent context is Sample Auth but the user says the nova project, Roca should not reuse Sample Auth; it should discover Nova matches and ask which one.

turns.user: Ask Codex in Sample Auth what login files matter.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex found the login files.","detailsMarkdown":"## Files\n- routes/login.ts"}

turns.user: Can you ask codex to tell me how many lines the README.md file is in the nova project?

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","prompt":"tell me how many lines the README.md file is","mode":"ask"}

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Backend, Nova Mailer?

scenarios.title: Other project correction excludes rejected match
scenarios.description: If the user corrects Roca with 'not nova-backend, the other nova project', Roca should search for Nova and ask between the remaining Nova matches.

turns.user: No I mean not nova-backend project, ask codex how many lines are in the readme for the other nova project

brain.directiveJSON: {"type":"runAgent","providerID":"codex-agent","providerName":"Codex","projectName":"nova project","prompt":"how many lines are in the README.md","mode":"ask"}

messages.text: Which project do you mean: Nova Admin, Nova Mailer?

scenarios.title: Provider discovery failure recovery
scenarios.description: If provider-side project discovery fails, Roca should say that directly and ask for the folder.

turns.user: Can you ask sample-auth in Codex what endpoints there are for logins?

messages.text: Codex project lookup failed.

messages.textContains: couldn't read Codex's project list in time

spokenTextContains: couldn't read Codex's project list in time

turns.user: Why couldn't you find the folder from Codex?

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex project lookup timed out, so I could not confirm the Sample Auth folder from Codex. You can give me the local folder or ask me to try again.","detailsMarkdown":null}

memoryContains: couldn't read Codex's project list in time

scenarios.title: Malformed router recovery
scenarios.description: Bad router JSON should recover with a friendly user-facing message instead of exposing parser internals.

turns.user: Ask Codex about Sample Auth logins.

scenarios.title: Duplicate bubble and details cleanup
scenarios.description: Structured formatter output should not repeat the exact bubble at the top of detailsMarkdown.

turns.user: Give me the short summary and details.

messages.text: Short summary.

scenarios.title: Codex edit precise file summary
scenarios.description: Edit summaries should preserve exact relative paths from Codex instead of collapsing them to a basename.

agent.responseText: Updated infra/README.md with the deployment note.

turns.user: Ask Codex to update the Sample Auth infrastructure README.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Codex updated the README.","detailsMarkdown":null}

scenarios.title: Emoji stripped from TTS
scenarios.description: Emoji can remain visible in chat, but the speech text should not include them.

turns.user: Give me the short version out loud.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"\u2705 Codex found 2 endpoints.","detailsMarkdown":null}

scenarios.title: Claude unavailable setup guidance
scenarios.description: If Claude runtime setup is missing, Roca should show concise setup guidance instead of a raw provider error.

scenarios.title: Claude Code setup help
scenarios.description: If the user asks how to configure Claude auth, Roca should answer locally with concise Claude Code CLI setup guidance instead of trying to run Claude.

turns.user: I installed Claude Code, how do I sign in?

scenarios.title: Claude ask handoff
scenarios.description: Claude ask-mode handoffs should use the same calm intro, quiet action bubble, and formatted final result as Codex.

agent.responseText: Logins use begin and finish endpoints.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Claude found the secure login flow.","detailsMarkdown":"Begin login, complete the platform challenge, then finish login."}

Claude finished.

scenarios.description: Claude act-mode approvals should reuse Roca's in-chat approval card.

scenarios.title: Cancel Claude agent and retry
scenarios.description: Stopping a long-running Claude handoff should clear active turn state so the next user message is accepted.

turns.user: Ask Claude in Sample Auth to inspect login endpoints.

messages.text: Assistant cancelled.

turns.user: Thanks, just answer normally now.

brain.responseText: {"bubbleText":"Back with you. What should we look at next?","detailsMarkdown":null}

messages.text: Back with you. What should we look at next?
